zh.lifeSummary: Hugh Thorne於1554年出生在英國薩默塞特比特明斯特。他於1573年2月22日與Ede West在英國英格蘭薩默塞特陶頓結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1616年1月28日在英國英格蘭薩默塞特比特明斯特享年62歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭薩默塞特比特明斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Hugh Thorne于1554年出生在英国薩默塞特比特明斯特。他于1573年2月22日与Ede West在英国英格兰薩默塞特陶頓结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1616年1月28日在英国英格兰薩默塞特比特明斯特享年62岁，埋葬在英国英格兰薩默塞特比特明斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Thorne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: adopted for various Irish names containing an element meaning ‘thorn’. Compare   Thornton  .
Swedish (mainly Thörne): ornamental or perhaps topographic name from törne ‘rose bush’ or thorn, an ornamental spelling of torn ‘thorn bush’.

variants.name: Thorn
meanings: English and Danish: topographic name for someone who lived by a thorn bush or hedge (Old English, Old Norse thorn, Middle English thorn(e), torn(e)). The name is also found in Sweden.
English: habitational name from a place called with Old English, Old Norse thorn ‘thorn bush’ (see 1 above), for example Thorne in Kent, Somerset, and South Yorkshire.
North German and Danish: topographic name for someone who lived near a tower, from Middle Low German torn ‘tower’.

variants.name: Thurn
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Katharina, Milbert.

German: topographic name for someone who lived near a watchtower, or a metonymic occupational name for someone responsible for manning one, from Middle High German turn, turm ‘tower’. In some cases it may be a habitational name from any of the many places called with this word in the Rhineland, Bavaria, Austria, and Bohemia.
English (Warwickshire): variant of   Thorne  . This form of the surname is very rare in Britain and Ireland.

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: Hugh Thorne was born in 1554, in Pitminster, Somerset, England. He married Ede West on 22 February 1573, in Taunton, Somerset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 28 January 1616, in Pitminster, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 62, and was buried in Pitminster, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Hugh Thorne est né en 1554, à Pitminster, Somerset, Angleterre. Il a épousé Ede West le 22 février 1573, à Taunton, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 28 janvier 1616, à Pitminster, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterré à Pitminster, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Hugh Thorne wurde 1554 in Pitminster, Somerset, England geboren. Er hat am 22. Februar 1573 in Taunton, Somerset, England, United Kingdom Ede West geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 28. Januar 1616 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Pitminster, Somerset, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Pitminster, Somerset, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Hugh Thorne è nato il nel 1554 nel Pitminster, Somerset, England. Lui sposato Ede West il 22 febbraio 1573, nel Taunton, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 28 gennaio 1616, nel Pitminster, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolto a Pitminster, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Hugh Thorneは1554年にイギリスソメルセトピトミンスターで生まれた。1573年2月22日にイギリスイギリスソメルセトタウントンでEde Westと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1616年1月28日にイギリスイギリスソメルセトピトミンスターで62歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスソメルセトピトミンスターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh Thorne(은)는 1554년 영국 서머세트 피트민스터에서 태어났다. 그는 1573년 2월 22일 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 타운턴에서 Ede West(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1616년 1월 28일 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 피트민스터에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 피트민스터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Hugh Thorne nasceu em 1554, em Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Ede West em 22 de fevereiro de 1573, em Taunton, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 28 de janeiro de 1616, em Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 62 anos, e foi sepultado em Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Hugh Thorne родился в 1554 г. в местности питминстир, сомирсет, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Ede West 22 февраля 1573 г. в местности таунтон, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер 28 января 1616 г. в местности питминстир, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности питминстир, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Hugh Thorne nació en 1554, en Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra. Se casó con Ede West el 22 de febrero de 1573, en Taunton, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 28 de enero de 1616, en Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultado en Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
